title: Assessing RNA Integrity from FFPE Samples for Spatial Transcriptomics
author: Shuoshuo Wang
materials: silica membrane column-based RNA extraction kit

steps.title: Workstation Decontamination Set up a clean...
steps.instruction: Workstation Decontamination Set up a clean workspace for handling samples and reagents. Adhere to RNase-free working standards: Ensure all working surfaces, reagents, and plasticware are RNase-free. Minimize handling and avoid unnecessary exposure to environmental contaminants. Always wear disposable gloves and replace them frequently to maintain cleanliness.

steps.title: Spray and clean all working zones,...
steps.instruction: Spray and clean all working zones, benches, chemical hoods, pipettes, and reagent containers with a sodium hypochlorite-based decontamination reagent (e.g., RNaseZap or RNase AWAY) or 0.5% bleach.

steps.title: Wipe surfaces using a paper towel...
steps.instruction: Wipe surfaces using a paper towel treated with the decontamination reagent.

steps.title: Spray and clean again using 70% ethanol
steps.instruction: Spray and clean again using 70% ethanol.

steps.title: Rinse cleaned surfaces with RNase-free water and wipe dry
steps.instruction: Rinse cleaned surfaces with RNase-free water and wipe dry.

steps.title: Set the microtome to a section...
steps.instruction: Set the microtome to a section thickness of 10 µm or 20 µm depending on sample type. For collagen-rich samples, thin sections are preferred. Thicker sections can reduce RNA yield.

steps.title: Make sure microtome, blades, tools, and Eppendorf tubes are RNase-free
steps.instruction: Make sure microtome, blades, tools, and Eppendorf tubes are RNase-free. Reface the block and discard the first few sections exposed to the environment. Consider trimming of the peripheral paraffins. If paraffin occupies more than 30% of the section's surface area, trim away the excess paraffin using a blade before collecting. Minimize carryover paraffin to enhance RNA purity and prevent interference during extraction.

steps.title: Determine the number of sections needed...
steps.instruction: Determine the number of sections needed based on tissue size and availability. For small tissues (<1 cm): Use approximately 4 sections or more. For large tissues (>1 cm): Use 2 sections. Perform pilot-based optimization to determine the appropriate input material for specific tissue types and experimental requirements. Adjusting as needed for tissue type, cellularity, and cross-sectional area. RNA yield correlates with tissue cellularity, too. Necrotic tissues have low RNA abundance. Use larger sections or additional scrolls for tissues with inherently low RNA content.

steps.title: Place the tissue sections (scrolls) immediately...
steps.instruction: Place the tissue sections (scrolls) immediately into pre-labeled, pre-cooled, RNase-free microcentrifuge tubes. Sections curl naturally due to tension in the paraffin wax, thus called "curls" or "scrolls" If the scrolls can not immediately processed, store them at -80 °C .

steps.title: Use a commercial silica membrane column-based...
steps.instruction: Use a commercial silica membrane column-based RNA extraction kit, for example: RNeasy FFPE Kit (Qiagen), Catalog Number 73504 PureLink FFPE RNA Isolation Kit (Thermo Fisher) High Pure FFPE RNA Micro Kit (Roche) Maxwell RSC RNA FFPE Kit (for automated extraction) Follow the manufacturer's instructions for RNA extraction. Read the "Before start" section from "Guidelines & Warnings" first.

steps.title: Deparaffinization Preheat a thermal mixer or...
steps.instruction: Deparaffinization Preheat a thermal mixer or heat block to 56°C for use. If available, preheat a second thermal mixer or heat block to 80°C to minimize waiting time during the protocol. Do use the blue tinted Daparaffinization Solution (Qiagen #19093) for enhanced visibility of phase separation. Do not use Xylene or organic solvents as replacement due to low flash point and risk of explosion. Home-brew solution can be found in Appendix A of Qiagen RNeasy FFPE Handbook 07/2021

steps.title: Add 160 µL (for smaller samples)...
steps.instruction: Add 160 µL (for smaller samples) or 320 µL (for larger samples) of Deparaffinization Solution to the sample. Note that the amount of buffers used in following steps will need to match the choice here.

steps.title: Vortex vigorously for 00:00:10 , then...
steps.instruction: Vortex vigorously for 00:00:10 , then briefly centrifuge to bring the sample to the bottom of the tube.

steps.title: Incubate the sample at 56 °C...
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample at 56 °C for 00:03:00 to dissolve the paraffin.

steps.title: Vortex vigorously for 00:00:10 , then...
steps.instruction: Vortex vigorously for 00:00:10 , then briefly centrifuge to bring the sample to the bottom of the tube.

steps.title: Incubate the sample at 56 °C...
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample at 56 °C for 00:03:00 to dissolve the paraffin.

steps.title: Allow the sample to cool to Room temperature
steps.instruction: Allow the sample to cool to Room temperature . If the solution becomes waxy or solid after cooling (indicating insufficient Deparaffinization Solution or excess paraffin), add more Deparaffinization Solution and repeat the 56°C incubation.

steps.title: Lysis Lysis and incubation conditions designed...
steps.instruction: Lysis Lysis and incubation conditions designed to reverse formaldehyde-induced modifications of RNA. The lysis buffer facilitates RNA release from FFPE tissue samples while minimizing degradation. These conditions aim to maximize the recovery of usable RNA from FFPE samples.

steps.title: Add 150 µL (for smaller samples)...
steps.instruction: Add 150 µL (for smaller samples) or 240 µL (for larger samples) of Buffer PKD (Qiagen #1034963) to the sample. PKD stands for Proteinase K digestion.

steps.title: Mix thoroughly by vortexing
steps.instruction: Mix thoroughly by vortexing.

steps.title: Centrifuge the sample at 11000 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the sample at 11000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 to separate phases.

steps.title: Carefully add 10 µL of Proteinase...
steps.instruction: Carefully add 10 µL of Proteinase K, 2mL Qiagen Catalog #19131 to the lower, clear phase.

steps.title: Mix gently by pipetting up and down
steps.instruction: Mix gently by pipetting up and down. Do not vortex.

steps.title: If possible, preheat a second thermal...
steps.instruction: If possible, preheat a second thermal mixer or heat block to 80°C to minimize waiting time.

steps.title: If only one heating block is...
steps.instruction: If only one heating block is available, allow the sample to remain at room temperature (15–25°C) after the 56°C incubation until the block reaches 80°C. Ensure the heating block is at 80°C before starting the 15-minute incubation, as this step is critical for reversing crosslinks.

steps.title: If using a heating block without...
steps.instruction: If using a heating block without shaking functionality, briefly vortex the sample every 3–5 minutes during incubation.

steps.title: Prolonged incubation or higher temperatures may increase RNA fragmentation
steps.instruction: Prolonged incubation or higher temperatures may increase RNA fragmentation.

steps.title: DNA Removal
steps.instruction: DNA Removal

steps.title: Incubate the sample On ice for 00:03:00
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample On ice for 00:03:00 .

steps.title: Centrifuge at 20000 x g, 4 °C, for 00:15:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 20000 x g, 4 °C, for 00:15:00 .

steps.title: Carefully transfer the supernatant to a new RNase-free microcentrifuge tube
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer the supernatant to a new RNase-free microcentrifuge tube. Genomic DNA contamination is common in RNA preparations and appears as a smooth bump near or above 6000 bp on QC traces. If not removed, it will persist through RNA-seq library preparation and sequencing, contributing to background reads and inaccuracies. To prevent this, treat RNA samples with DNase before starting library preparation, ensuring proper cleanup or buffer exchange as needed. Avoid disturbing the pellet, which contains insoluble tissue debris and crosslinked DNA, as carryover can introduce impurities and cause significant errors in DV200 measurements.

steps.title: Add DNase Booster Buffer (10% of...
steps.instruction: Add DNase Booster Buffer (10% of the total sample volume, approximately 16 µL) and 10 µL DNase I stock solution to the supernatant.

steps.title: Mix by inverting the tube and...
steps.instruction: Mix by inverting the tube and briefly centrifuge to collect residual liquid from the tube walls. Do not vortex.

steps.title: Incubate the sample at Room temperature for 00:15:00
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample at Room temperature for 00:15:00

steps.title: Column loading and RNA purification If...
steps.instruction: Column loading and RNA purification If kit is new, prepare Buffer RPE by add 44 mL 100% ethanol to the bottle containing 11 mL Buffer RPE concentrate. Mix well and mark the checkbox on the bottle label.

steps.title: Add 320 µL Buffer RBC to...
steps.instruction: Add 320 µL Buffer RBC to the sample and mix thoroughly.

steps.title: Add 720 µL ethanol (100%) and mix well by pipetting
steps.instruction: Add 720 µL ethanol (100%) and mix well by pipetting. Do not centrifuge.

steps.title: Transfer 700 µL of the sample...
steps.instruction: Transfer 700 µL of the sample (including any precipitate) to a pre-labeled spin column placed in a 2 mL collection tube.

steps.title: Close the lid gently and centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Close the lid gently and centrifuge at no less than 8000 x g, °C, for 00:00:15 .

steps.title: Process Remaining Sample
steps.instruction: Process Remaining Sample: Repeat the loading and centrifugation process until the entire sample has passed through the spin column.

steps.title: Discard the flow-through and reuse the...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow-through and reuse the collection tube for subsequent steps. it is critical not to collect RBC containing flow-through in a waste container with bleach or any chlorine-containing compounds, and appropriate safety precautions should be taken when handling these chemicals. Otherwise, guanidine hydrochloride in RBC will release chlorine gas upon reaction with hypochlorite. Exposure to chlorine gas can lead to severe lung damage or even death.

steps.title: Add 500 µL Buffer RPE to the spin column
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL Buffer RPE to the spin column.

steps.title: Close the lid gently and centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Close the lid gently and centrifuge at no less than 8000 x g, °C, for 00:00:15 .

steps.title: Discard the flow-through and reuse the...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow-through and reuse the collection tube for the next step.

steps.title: Repeat Washing
steps.instruction: Repeat Washing: Repeat steps 10.7–10.9, but centrifuge for 00:02:00 instead of 15 seconds.

steps.title: After centrifugation, carefully remove the spin...
steps.instruction: After centrifugation, carefully remove the spin column from the collection tube, ensuring the column does not contact the flow-through to avoid ethanol carryover contamination.

steps.title: RNA Elution and Drying
steps.instruction: RNA Elution and Drying

steps.title: Place the spin column in a...
steps.instruction: Place the spin column in a new 2 mL collection tube.

steps.title: Open the lid of the spin...
steps.instruction: Open the lid of the spin column and centrifuge at full speed for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Discard the collection tube with the flow-through
steps.instruction: Discard the collection tube with the flow-through.

steps.title: To prevent damage to the spin...
steps.instruction: To prevent damage to the spin column lids, ensure there is at least one empty position between columns in the centrifuge. Orient the lids in the opposite direction of the rotor's rotation (e.g., if the rotor rotates clockwise, orient the lids counterclockwise). Proper drying of the spin column membrane is crucial to remove residual ethanol, which could interfere with downstream reactions. Keeping the lids open during centrifugation ensures no ethanol is carried over during RNA elution.

steps.title: Transfer the spin column to a new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the spin column to a new 1.5 mL collection tube.

steps.title: Add 14–30 µL RNase-free water directly...
steps.instruction: Add 14–30 µL RNase-free water directly onto the spin column membrane. Wait 00:00:30 to prime. Eluting with smaller volumes of water results in higher RNA concentrations, but may reduce RNA yield. The spin column has a 2 µL dead volume, so elution with 14 µL RNase-free water will yield approximately 12 µL of eluate.

steps.title: Close the lid gently and centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Close the lid gently and centrifuge at full speed for 00:01:00 to elute the RNA. Save 4 µL for QC and place on ice. If the analysis cannot be performed immediately, store purified and unused RNA at 80 °C for long-term storage.

steps.title: Keep RNA samples On ice at...
steps.instruction: Keep RNA samples On ice at all times during the analysis procedure. For quantitation, use a Qubit 4 Fluorometer to measure RNA concentration to determine the appropriate dilution for loading the instrument and gel type. Qubit RNA HS assay has a quantitatie range from 4 to 200 ng. Qubit RNA HS (High Sensitivity) assay Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #Q32852 Pay attention to the quantitative range for : 500-10000 pg/µL for High Sensitivity RNA ScreenTape. So the minimal concentration will be 0.5 ng/uL. The DV200, as a %, might be artificially inflated due to input in the non-quantitative range.​ Do not use NanoDrop for quantitation due to: Inaccuracy: NanoDrop measures sample purity, not the accurate concentration. Low Sensitivity: It is unsuitable for samples with low concentration. The Qubit RNA IQ Assay does not have the necessary sensitivity (>500ng) for small samples. Qubit 4 (Fluorometer, Invitrogen, Q33238)

steps.title: DV200 score can be measured using...
steps.instruction: DV200 score can be measured using instruments like TapeStation (>1000pg, High Sensitivity RNA ScreenTape, # 5067-5579 ), BioAnalyzer (>50pg with RNA 6000 Pico, # 5067-1513 ), or Fragment Analyzer (>100 pg with Agilent DNF-472, #DNF-472-0500). TapeStation (Agilent, G2991AA, https://www.agilent.com/en/product/tapestation-automated-electrophoresis/tapestation-instruments/4200-tapestation-system-228263 ) Bioanalyzer (Bioanalyzer, Agilent, G2991AA, Any bioanalyzer will suffice., https://www.agilent.com/en/product/bioanalyzer-automated-electrophoresis/bioanalyzer-instrument/2100-bioanalyzer-instrument-228250 , https://s3.amazonaws.com/pr-journal/bfif3sqe.jpg ) Fragment Analyzer (capillary based nucleic acid fragment size separation, Agilent, M5311AA, https://www.agilent.com/en/product/automated-electrophoresis/fragment-analyzer-systems/fragment-analyzer-systems/5300-fragment-analyzer-system-365721 )

steps.title: Calculate DV200 following the manufacturer's instructions
steps.instruction: Calculate DV200 following the manufacturer's instructions. For TapeStation: https://www.agilent.com/en/promotions/tapestation-dv200-determination For BioAnalyzer: https://www.agilent.com/en/promotions/2100-bioanalyzer-dv200-determination For Fragment Analyzer: https://www.agilent.com/en/promotions/fragment-analyzer-dv200-determination Assay requirements vary, but as a general guideline, a DV200 below 30% critically impacts performance and represents the minimum acceptable threshold, while a DV200 above 50% is considered adequate.
